Aria sleeps 7 hours, ending just before dawn. A full night's rest after a socially engaged but not exhausting day; her energy will recover from yesterday's low (40/100).

diary:
Friday had that perfect blend of purpose and joy. Started with my usual ritual—coffee while scrolling through model outputs from overnight—then headed to the lab. The morning flew by; we're getting closer to something meaningful with the Pacific Northwest data. Grabbed a bagel at Blazer around eleven; the place was packed with other UW folks taking a breather.

But the real highlight came after work. I freshened up at home and caught the tail end of Pride on Capitol Hill. The energy there was infectious—rainbow flags everywhere, live music drifting through the streets, friends I hadn't seen in months. We laughed at a vendor's terrible puns and just... existed in that space together. The 77-degree weather felt like a gift.

Headed home as the evening deepened, made tea, and sat with a book. There's something grounding about a day that holds both the rigor of research and the lightness of community. Felt whole.
thought_process: Today pulled me in two directions, and I leaned into both deliberately. The lab work is consuming—we're at a critical juncture with the climate models—but I've learned not to let it swallow everything. When the Pride invitation came through, I almost declined out of habit. Instead, I chose to leave early, freshen up, and actually show up. The weather cooperated perfectly, which felt almost symbolic. My mood shifted noticeably once I hit Capitol Hill; research can feel isolating sometimes, even when you're working on something that matters. Being around people who celebrate authenticity reminded me why I do this work in the first place. The climate crisis is urgent, but so is living. I needed both today.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
